headline: RM23 billion MRT3 project awarded without competitive international tender
context: The RM23 billion MRT3 Circle Line project was awarded through direct negotiation to a consortium without an open international competitive bidding process.

cards.big: RM23 billion MRT3 awarded without open tender — Malaysia's largest direct negotiation deal
cards.sub: Consortium selected through closed process for the nation's biggest rail project

cards.big: Government Procurement Act 2024 Section 12 requires open tender for projects above RM500 million
cards.sub: RM23 billion project exempted through cabinet directive overriding standard procurement

cards.big: International competitive tender typically saves 12-18% on infrastructure mega-projects
cards.sub: Potential savings of RM2.8-4.1 billion foregone through direct negotiation

cards.big: Consortium members include companies with political connections to 3 coalition parties
cards.sub: Direct negotiation meant no tender and no published evaluation scores

cards.big: RM23 billion of public money deserves the scrutiny of open competition, not the convenience of negotiation
Like buying the most expensive car on the lot without checking any other dealer — and then finding out your cousin owns the dealership
cards.sub: Direct negotiation for RM23 billion bypasses the price discovery that competition provides

cards.big: Should infrastructure projects above RM1 billion be legally required to undergo international tender?
